intro.title: Discover Victory Square
intro.description: Walking into Victory Square, you will be enveloped by a rich historical atmosphere and architectural beauty. This cobblestone pedestrian corridor reads like a European architectural history book. On a summer evening, sunset gilds the facades with golden light. Baroque reliefs and Art Nouveau ironwork appear exquisite. The central fountain splashes, and the She-Wolf statue tells Romania story. When night falls, lights outline building contours, transforming the square into a romantic nightlife stage.

visitGuide.title: Visit Tips
items: Weekday mornings or evenings for fewer crowds
Opera House and Cathedral require separate tickets

alsoKnownAs.title: Highlights
items: Birthplace of 1989 Revolution, Cradle of Freedom
Cultural capital of Western Romania, Little Vienna

sections.title: Historical Significance
sections.content: Victory Square originated as a parade ground in the 18th century and was redesigned in 1910 by architect Ludwig von Ybl. Orthodox Cathedral completed in the 1930s. On December 20, 1989, the Romanian Revolution erupted. Tens of thousands gathered, declaring Timisoara the first free city. The square was renamed Victory Square with a cross monument. Over centuries, it transformed from a royal parade ground to a symbol of freedom.

sections.title: Architectural Features
sections.content: 480-meter pedestrian square bounded by National Opera House (north) and Orthodox Cathedral (south). Baroque palaces, Vienna Secession apartments, Neo-Renaissance theaters flank both sides. Northern section features Neo-Renaissance, Baroque, Vienna Secession. Southern Cathedral displays Neo-Moorish architecture with red brick and colorful domes.

sections.title: Cultural Significance
sections.content: Romania national spiritual symbol. During 1989 Revolution, tens of thousands declared Timisoara the first free city. Hosts JazzTM Festival, Timfloralis Exhibition, Opera Festival, Christmas Market. Surrounded by museums, galleries, theaters, cafes.

sections.title: Core Experience
sections.content: Walking into Victory Square, you are enveloped by history and architectural beauty. Sunset gilds facades with golden light. Baroque reliefs and Art Nouveau ironwork appear exquisite. Fountain splashes, She-Wolf stands silently. At night, lights outline contours, transforming the square into a romantic nightlife stage.
